disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A lump on the side of the nose has been slowly growing for about two years, starting as a small shiny spot
trigger_question_examples: How long has the lump been there?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started as a little shiny spot on the side of the nose about two years ago and it has slowly got bigger since then.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lump has a small central crater/sore with a raised, pearly, shiny rolled edge and fine tiny blood vessels visible across the surface
trigger_question_examples: What does the lump look like up close?
Can you describe the edges and the middle of it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It is a shiny raised lump with a little dip or sore in the middle, and the raised edge looks pearly with tiny blood vessels running over it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lesion bleeds easily and repeatedly with minor trauma or towel drying and today has been oozing continuously for hours
trigger_question_examples: How long has it been bleeding today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It has bled a few times over the last few months whenever it gets knocked or I dry my face, and today it has just kept oozing for about four hours.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient takes low-dose aspirin 100 mg daily
trigger_question_examples: Are you on any blood-thinning medicines?
What medications do you take each day?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I take one low-dose aspirin, 100 mg, every day.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lump has never been painful or itchy
trigger_question_examples: Is the lump painful or itchy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it has never really hurt or itched — it just bleeds.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lesion is on chronically sun-exposed facial skin in a frail elderly person
trigger_question_examples: Where exactly on the face is it?
Has that area had a lot of sun over the years?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It is right on the side of the nose, an area that has had plenty of sun over the years.
